raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "illustrating the mechanism and consequences of autosuggestion through contrasting medical pronouncements",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "a doctor's title confers inherent suggestive power over patients",
    "verbal statements by authority figures directly generate autosuggestions in the recipient's mind",
    "negative autosuggestions can produce objectively harmful physiological or psychological outcomes",
    "positive reframing of prognosis can produce unexpectedly favorable health results",
    "the mind's response to suggestion operates independently of or beyond purely material medical interventions"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent within a framework that treats suggestion as a causal force; the contrast between negative and positive cases follows logically from the stated premise",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "autosuggestion must be a real causal pathway capable of altering health trajectories; the speaker's intent must be to guide ethical use of verbal influence rather than to replace evidence-based medicine",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the described effects must be attributed solely to suggestion while ignoring biomedical factors, statistical regression, or placebo controls; or the claim must be taken as license to withhold factual diagnoses"
}
